_comment_top: Example config for gsa_force_extractor.py. Copy this file, rename to e.g. gsa_extractor_config.json, edit, then run: python gsa_force_extractor.py gsa_extractor_config.json

units._comment: Output units for forces and moments. GSA model units are detected automatically at run time.

signs._comment: Each flag describes GSA's convention. The script flips iff GSA disagrees with the app (Pu < 0 = compression, Mu < 0 = hogging). Defaults assume the typical GSA convention: tension-positive Fx (matches app -> no flip) and hogging-positive Myy/Mzz (differs from app -> flip).

jobs.name: Pier cap mid-span (strength)
jobs._comment_location: Pick exactly one of: element / elements / group / property. Position is 0..1 along the element local x-axis, OR the string 'max' to capture the position where each enveloped force is largest.
jobs._comment_axes: Map app-side force names to GSA local axes. Defaults: Pu=Fx, Mu=Myy, Vu=Fz, Tu=Mxx (the convention for typical GSA beam elements where Myy is the principal bending moment).
jobs._comment_envelopes: Each rule emits top_n rows for that ranking. Coexistent forces in the same row come from the same GSA permutation (and same position when 'max' is used).

jobs.name: Pile group reactions (summed)
jobs._comment: Set sum_across_elements=true to add force components across every element in the selection per (permutation, position). Useful for total reaction at a pile cap, total shear in a deck cross-section, etc. The Element column shows e.g. 'sum(PileGroupA)'. Requires consistent local axes across the summed elements.

jobs.name: Pier cap group example
jobs._comment: Group/property selectors WITHOUT sum_across_elements emit one block of rows per element. Useful for sweeping a whole component independently.
